void global_act(Character *ch, const Str.brief: Broadcasts a message to all connected characters who meet specific visibility and communication criteria.
void global_act(Character *ch, const Str.details: The function iterates through all active descriptors and sends a formatted message to each character who is currently playing, is not the source character, and does not have communication restrictions specified by 'nocomm_bits'. It respects invisibility settings based on 'despite_invis' and visibility rules determined by 'can_see_who'. The message is displayed with specified color and boldness, and the character's color settings are restored afterward. This function effectively enables global communication or announcements within the game environment, considering individual character states and permissions.

params.ch: Pointer to the source Character initiating the message; can be null for system-wide messages.
params.message: The message string to be broadcasted, potentially containing placeholders for dynamic content.
params.despite_invis: Flag indicating whether invisibility should be ignored when determining message recipients.
params.color: Integer representing the color code to be used for message display.
params.nocomm_bits: Flags specifying communication restrictions; characters with matching flags will not receive the message.

void global_act(Character *ch, const Str.returns: Void; the function performs its operation by sending messages to appropriate characters without returning a value.
void global_act(Character *ch, const Str.name: global_act
void global_act(Character *ch, const Str.argsstring: (Character *ch, const String &message, int despite_invis, int color, const Flags &nocomm_bits)

void talk_auction(const String &argument.brief: Broadcasts an auction message to all appropriate characters in the game.
void talk_auction(const String &argument.details: The function iterates through all active descriptors, identifying the associated characters. For each character, it checks if they are currently playing and whether their censor flags permit message viewing; if so, it performs a swear word check on the message argument. Characters who are not in quiet mode and have not disabled auction communications receive the message, which is formatted with a specific color and prefix, then reset to default color settings. This function ensures that only eligible characters receive auction messages and that swear words are filtered according to censor flags.

params.argument: The message string to be broadcasted in the auction channel.

void talk_auction(const String &argument.returns: Void; the function does not return a value but sends formatted auction messages to eligible characters.
void talk_auction(const String &argument.name: talk_auction

void channel(Character *ch, const String.brief: Handles communication through various channels for a character, including toggling, messaging, and visibility checks.
void channel(Character *ch, const String.details: This function manages the sending and toggling of different communication channels for a character in the game. It checks for clan membership restrictions, toggles channel states if no message argument is provided, enforces communication restrictions such as quiet mode and revocations, processes special cases like 'who' queries, and dispatches messages to appropriate recipients based on visibility, permissions, and channel-specific rules. It supports different channel types, including immortal talk, clan, pray, flame, and others, with special formatting and filtering logic for each. The function ensures that messages are only sent to characters who are allowed to see or hear them, respecting ignore lists, censor flags, and other conditions.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character initiating the channel command.
params.argument: The message or command argument string provided by the character.
params.channel: Integer representing the specific communication channel to be used.

void channel(Character *ch, const String.returns: Void; the function performs message dispatching and state toggling without returning a value.
void channel(Character *ch, const String.name: channel

void channel_who(Character *ch, const St.brief: Displays a list of players who have a specified communication channel enabled.
void channel_who(Character *ch, const St.details: This function outputs the names of all characters currently connected and actively using the specified channel, excluding certain categories based on permissions and flags. It first checks if the caller is an NPC and returns immediately if so. For player characters, it sends a header message indicating the channel being listed, then iterates through all connected descriptors to identify and display characters who have the channel enabled, considering visibility, permissions, and specific flags such as 'no whisper' or 'quiet' modes. Certain channels, like 'no flame', also check for additional censor flags to filter out censored players. The function ensures only appropriate characters are listed based on the channel's context and the caller's permissions.

params.ch: Pointer to the Character object representing the caller, used to determine permissions and visibility.
params.channelname: A String representing the name of the channel being queried, used for display purposes.
params.channel: A Flags::Bit value indicating the specific communication channel to list, used for filtering characters.
params.custom: An integer used to specify color or display options when sending output to the caller.

void channel_who(Character *ch, const St.returns: Void; the function outputs the list directly to the caller's communication channel and does not return a value.
void channel_who(Character *ch, const St.name: channel_who
void channel_who(Character *ch, const St.argsstring: (Character *ch, const String &channelname, const Flags::Bit &channel, int custom)

void wiznet(const String &string, Charac.brief: Sends a notification message to all eligible immortal characters with specific wiznet flags, excluding certain flags and below a minimum rank.
void wiznet(const String &string, Charac.details: The wiznet function iterates through all active descriptors representing connected characters. It filters characters based on several conditions: the character must be currently playing, an immortal, have the WIZ_ON flag set, possess the specified wiznet flags, not have any of the skip flags, and meet the minimum rank requirement. Additionally, the message is not sent to the character initiating the call. If the recipient has the WIZ_PREFIX flag, a prefix is added before the message. The message content is formatted and dispatched to each qualifying character's output buffer. This function is typically used for administrative or debugging notifications visible only to immortal characters with appropriate flags.

params.string: The message text to be sent, formatted with placeholders for dynamic content.
params.ch: The character who initiated the message, excluded from receiving the wiznet notification.
params.obj: An object associated with the message context, if any.
params.flag: A set of flags indicating which wiznet flags must be present for recipients.
params.flag_skip: A set of flags indicating which wiznet flags should cause recipients to be excluded.
params.min_rank: The minimum rank required for characters to receive the message.

void wiznet(const String &string, Charac.returns: Void; the function does not return a value but dispatches messages to eligible characters.
void wiznet(const String &string, Charac.name: wiznet
void wiznet(const String &string, Charac.argsstring: (const String &string, Character *ch, Object *obj, const Flags &flag, const Flags &flag_skip, int min_rank)
